matcher.subject: Science (086)
matcher.coveragePreview: chemical substances and their properties class x science students reactivity series ionic compounds corrosion chemical substances and their properties introduction to chemical properties why do some metals rust while others don't? how do atoms form bonds? the reactivity series reactivity series metals are arranged in decreasing order of reactivity. highly reactive metals react vigorously with water/acid. less reactive metals do not react with dilute acids. formation of ionic bonds ionic compounds metals lose electrons (oxidation). non-metals gain electrons (reduction). opposite charges attract to form ionic bonds. corrosion and its prevention corrosion corrosion: deterioration of metals due to reaction with air/moisture. iron rusts (fe2o3), silver tarnishes (ag2s). prevention: painting, galvanization, oiling.
